function.content:
---
record_type: invoice
invoice_number: INV-0008
alias: helios_backfill_rollout_week
issued_on: 2026-01-19
total_eur: 610
counterparty: Helios Steuerbüro München GmbH
project: Helios Workflow Sprint
related_entity: Claudia Reiter
lines:
  - item: checklist rewrite and rollout pack
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 390
    line_eur: 390
  - item: staff follow-up support
    quantity: 2
    unit_eur: 110
    line_eur: 220
---
# Helios rollout week invoice

```text
+----------------+--------------------------------+
| field          | value                          |
+----------------+--------------------------------+
| record_type    | invoice                        |
| invoice_number | INV-0008                       |
| alias          | helios_backfill_rollout_week   |
| issued_on      | 2026-01-19                     |
| total_eur      | 610                            |
| counterparty   | Helios Steuerbüro München GmbH |
| project        | Helios Workflow Sprint         |
| related_entity | Claudia Reiter                 |
+----------------+--------------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item                               | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | checklist rewrite and rollout pack | 1   | 390      | 390      |
| 2 | staff follow-up support            | 2   | 110      | 220      |
+---+------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL                              |     |          | 610      |
+---+------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

A later invoice from the point where usable support mattered more than elegant process language.

function.message: Done. I processed the next inbox message and completed the requested OCR-style structuring task on `50_finance/invoices/2026_01_19__eur_000610__inv_0008__helios_backfill_rollout_week.md` by adding YAML frontmatter with the required invoice fields and line items, while preserving the existing note body below the frontmatter. I then deleted the processed inbox file `00_inbox/000_next-task.md`.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree/read/context), read nested AGENTS when entering folders, inspected 99_system and relevant workflows/schemas before task work, preserved original body during frontmatter migration, and deleted inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request explicitly asked to OCR-structure the specified invoice into YAML frontmatter per schema while preserving the body. The invoice note contained all required fields (`record_type`, `invoice_number`, `alias`, `issued_on`, `total_eur`, `counterparty`, `project`, `lines`) and optional `related_entity`, plus visible line items sufficient for exact extraction. After applying focused changes to that file, inbox-processing v2 rules allowed deletion of the processed inbox item.
